title: The Big Muck Problem: One Pile at a Time
summary: When Bert and Ernie’s neighborhood becomes overwhelmed by spring junk and a massive mud puddle, a clever young visitor named Izzy shares her Nana’s wisdom: tackle problems 'one at a time.' Joining forces with a hard-hat-wearing Minnie Mouse and her powerful yellow digger, the friends embark on a mission to clean up everything from the garden to the sidewalk. From fixing holes in the street to finding books on the moon, it’s a mucky adventure that proves no problem is too big when you have good friends and a bit of patience.

highlightQuote.text: But you can try it one at a time! I learned that from my nana. I learned that from my nana!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: [Singing]

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Brush brush brush, I'm combing your hair, even your beard if you're in this chair.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, that rhymed, I loved that. Can you do another rhyme like that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I cannot. I don't know how to finish stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, oh, well, the stars are on. We've just got to turn out the big light and the little light. And now it's time for stars and stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, do you want to come over and sit in my lap for a bit?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I forgot to get the blue timer. Come over. I forgot to get the blue timer.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Blue timer is set.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hi. High five. Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Whoa.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: High five.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Tell me some stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I always dress in you... now you me... just wait to get cozy... get cozy in your lap... tell your dad and I love you, Dada.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, do you like making robots?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I do, do you?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I do. And I like to help with that part where you put the little... with the little heart with the edges on the robots. I like to help with that part.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I love that part. All right. Now, tonight's story is called—

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hi!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, no, it's called 'Spring Cleaning at the Castle'.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, not 'Spring Cleaning at the Castle'! It's 'Spring Cleaning at Bert and Ernie's' with Bert and Ernie.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Rubber Ducky, too!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Rubber Ducky. Once upon a time, Izzy the Kiddo was visiting Bert and Ernie's house.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Rubber Ducky's house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And it was Rubber Ducky's house, too. And Bert said to everyone, 'Well, I'm so glad you're here, because it's just beginning to be spring, which is a perfect time to clean up. And we have a little bit of a problem with what you might call junk. Now, Izzy, where would you like to start?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, Bert and Ernie, yesterday when I was doing the junk activity, I saw more junk and a big junk in a trash bag and then I put in my old bag when I was junking yesterday.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow. Well, it's good to know, said Bert, that you have some experience with cleaning junk because we have a lot here in our house, too. There's some junk out in the garden, there's some junk here in the kitchen...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Where else?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And there's junk in the living room. There's junk all over the place.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ugh! Well, I happen to have no broom up here.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No broom? Oh, Ernie, could you help us find a broom? 'Oh, sure, Bert!'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But I have one broom downstairs!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, do you want to go run and get it, Izzy?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, I don't have it. I left it. So you better hand me a different broom.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Ernie said, 'Oh, yep, there's one right here in the closet, I think.' Open up the closet door and there was a big crash. And Bert said, 'What are you doing in there, Ernie?' And out came flying a big bouncy ball, a couple of jackets, and a bucket fell over, and a whole bunch of toys rolled right out of the closet. And Ernie popped his head out and said, 'Oh, here it is!' and he swung the broom right out. And Bert said, 'Ernie, what are you doing? There's junk all over the floor now. What are we going to do?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Sweep it, baby!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's a good idea, Izzy. 'And the dustpan!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, here's the broom and here's the dustpan, said Ernie. You can go ahead with that, Izzy. And then what does Izzy do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She tried sweeping into the... into the dustpan, but the trash didn't make it into the dustpan!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It didn't? What happened?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Then Bert tried it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Then Bert tried it. And then what happened?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bert swept all the trash.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, he swept all the trash into the dustpan. And then Bert said, 'Okay, everybody, it's time to go clean up the junk outside in our garden.' And—

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, he... Bert already did the junking out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's right. So they opened the back door and they went out into the garden. And what kind of junk did they see there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was junk... all kinds of junk.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Were there any rabbit poops?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep. He also had that.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so what did Izzy the Kiddo do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bert tried sweeping all the junk outside.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He swept it and he swept it and he swept it. And he made it into a big pile. And then what did they do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They... they... they went back inside and threw the junk in the trash.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They went back in and threw it in the trash. And then Bert said, 'Wow, thanks for your help so far. We've cleaned up almost everything. But now we have one more problem and it's a really big problem.' And he said, 'Come with me and I'll show you.' And he opened the front door.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Where is your problem, Bert?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, let me show you! And everyone walked out onto the front step of the house and looked down at the sidewalk in front of the house and you know what there was? There was a huge, yucky, mucky, gucky mud puddle! And he said, 'Ugh, there's so much mud. And if I stick my foot in there, I get stuck in the mud. And it's so yucky!'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, sweep that with your broom, Bert!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, you know what? I tried that, but it's too big to sweep it. There's so much mud and muck and yucky—

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But you can try it one at a time! I learned that from my nana. I learned that from my nana!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, one at a time? That's a good idea. How would we do that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Maybe you could do one muck pile at a time.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: One muck pile at a time? Okay, let's try it. And so Bert swept up a little bit of the muck into a bucket and then he threw it in the trash. And then he came back and he said, 'Okay, I did one. Am I done?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, there's so much more muck!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: There's a lot more muck, you're right. And then he swept up another one squelch because that was a deep, squilchy, squishy, squashy puddle of muck. And he filled up another bucket and he threw it away and he said, 'Wah, this is hard work. Am I done yet now, Izzy?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I am? But there's still so much more muck and gunk here.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But you have more problems everywhere! Yeah, in your house, a hole in the street! You have junk on the roof of your house! You have you have... some tape stuck on your radiator! You have the picture... there's a picture stuck on here... and a—

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A picture where?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Stuck on here! And there's a book on the moon!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: There's a book on the moon!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah! And there's a mouse, a toy mouse Mickey can help you clean everything! And the stairs! There's a picture, there's a toy table, that's in the left of the wall, and it got broken on the sidewalk! And the paper also got broken!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We do have a lot of things to clean up and to fix, but I think your advice is really good to do a little at a time.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And you sweep back the junk! And your blanket! And on the... you have a hole in your pillow, and your flashlight has a hole!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And as Izzy was discussing these things with Bert and Ernie, everyone turned to look down the street because they heard a loud sound. It was rumbly and it was getting a little louder and a little louder because a big vehicle was coming down the street. And do you know what kind of vehicle it was? It was a yellow digger driving down the street. And who was driving it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was Minnie!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was Minnie Mouse wearing a hard hat because she's a digger driver. And she pulled up in front of Bert and Ernie's house and she said, 'Hello, everybody!'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Minnie! Bert and Ernie have a lot of problems on the street and everywhere!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh, they do? How can I help?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Maybe you could clean up some one at a time like I learned from my nana!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh, clean them up, clean them up one at a time? That's a great idea!'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, I learned that from my nana a long time ago!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Your nana must be very wise. Well, let's just try cleaning up this one thing right here because I see right next to where I parked my digger a big, yucky pile of muck. But I can scoop that right up all in one go with my digger, it's a big scoop. Ready?'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It scooped up all the muck all in one go and then it lifted it right up. And she said, 'Well, I better take this somewhere where I can dump it out safely.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You can dump it in this... chair!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh yeah, I'll dump it on that giant cup! Thanks, Izzy!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so she drove off with her digger to dump out the muck and the yuck and the guck safely so that it wouldn't be in a big puddle in front of Bert and Ernie's house. And everyone said—

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Minnie, there's other problems that you have to fix!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Minnie said, 'Well, we solved this one and maybe we'll solve the other problems another time.' Because that was the end of tonight's story for Stars and—

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Minnie is going off in her digger to do the other problems!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yep. 'Bye-bye, Minnie!' And she says, 'Bye-bye, everyone!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Bert says 'Bye-bye,' and Ernie says 'Bye-bye.' And then what did Izzy say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bye-bye, Minnie Mouse!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Bye-bye, Minnie Mouse. And that is the end. All cold.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm going to take a bath.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right. Step down. Thank you.

characters.name: Bert
characters.description: Stepping from the quiet ink of a library book to join the trek into the deep dark, this classic figure from a shared basement apartment maintains the rhythmic precision and meditative focus of a sleep-story weaver. Formerly the host of grand pigeon galas who folded his roommate Ernie’s vibrant chaos into serene harmony, he now applies a caretaker’s grace to the encroaching shadows. Whether smoothing mud-stained hems into crisp geometries or sorting through the abyss’s chaotic clutter, he treats the exploration of the unknown as a soothing ritual—a singular, exacting discipline of folding the world’s wilder reaches back into a state of restorative order.

characters.name: Ernie
characters.description: Bert’s classic companion and roommate moves through their shared world with the jittery stealth of a mouse, their impatient haste betraying them with a trail of unintended squeaks as they assist in the search for the lost pearl. Often found in a futile pursuit of order—whether frantically sweeping thick mud or overwhelmed by a cascade of forgotten toys—this iconic figure remains a soul perpetually caught between domestic chaos and a sudden drenching from a garden hose. Yet, on the long journey for the treasure, their nervous energy settles into a rhythmic wonder; they trade the mess of the earth for the fixed, silent patterns of the cosmos, weaving evocative stories of the stars to guide their path.

characters.name: Rubber Ducky
characters.description: A resident of the house who participates in the spring cleaning adventure.

characters.name: Izzy the Kiddo
characters.description: Once a quiet dreamer sketching from a recovery bed, this brave explorer now leads her companions through Hattie’s workshop and the lightless abyss, guided by a beacon of special light. Clad in a rugged driving suit alongside Bluey and Fincat’s Mouse, she wields heavy hammers and a magic wand with logistical precision inherited from her Nana. From trading plastic bricks for rare mushrooms to orchestrating neighborhood cleanups, she balances specialized gear with a playful spirit. Choosing the spark of a game over the gravity of a traditional quest, she ensures her radiant creativity and guiding light remain her most powerful tools for navigating the unknown.

characters.name: Minnie Mouse
characters.description: A gracious mouse and tireless caretaker who bridges the gap between her charming storefront, rugged construction sites, and the shimmering cosmos. Whether she is curating fine art supplies, clearing giant mud puddles as an expert digger driver, or piloting her vibrantly colorful rocket ship through the stars, she tends to her community with a unique blend of delicate charm, heavy-duty utility, and an explorer’s boundless spirit.

characters.name: Nana
characters.description: The dignified mole matriarch and grandmother to Hattie, Pistis, and Isabella, this pillar of watchful wisdom resides at the end of the train line, tackling life’s problems "one at a time." From engineering mouse-scale Lego structures to the meticulous restoration of dollhouses using patient glue and tiny pencils, her steady paws bridge the gap between architectural feats and delicate artistry. Whether discerning potential as a trolley passenger or welcoming weary travelers into her sanctuary, her quiet authority is so legendary that she graces the cover of Hattie’s book in a rare, peaceful slumber. Even in rest, she remains the heart of their world—a steady warmth anchoring every journey’s end, her sharp eyes softening for Izzy’s mushroom-textured crafts.

characters.name: Mickey Mouse
characters.description: Minnie’s musical companion and star-bound co-pilot is an industrious toy mouse who returns from school to mend the broken and fill the silence of the void with song. While he remains Izzy’s imagined confidant—a silent listener to her whispered anxieties regarding dwindling supplies—he also finds joy in companionship, proudly showcasing his intricate Lego creations to a trusted friend amidst the stars.

places.name: Bert
places.description: The primary setting where spring cleaning is desperately needed.

places.name: Ernie's House
places.description: The primary setting where spring cleaning is desperately needed.

places.name: The Garden
places.description: Behind the house, a junk-strewn expanse gives way to the tangled thicket, a strategic blind overlooking the decimated vegetable patch. Once used to ambush the pool noodle nibbler, it now guards the hollowed stalks of a woodchuck’s feast, its floor a messy tapestry of discarded refuse and rabbit droppings.

places.name: The Moon
places.description: Visible from Earth as a pockmarked sphere of gray, white, and black, this low-gravity waypoint for sun-bound crews is a playground of ancient space rocks and leaping Floofers; its silver dust now serves as a silent sanctuary for a misplaced book and the long-lost mother cat.

places.name: The Sidewalk
places.description: The winding path where Mama Mole and Disco forage for nature’s treasures now begins at a formidable threshold: a massive, sticky mud puddle that sprawls across the front of the house, turning their usual starting point into a thick, gooey mire.

places.name: The Street
places.description: The sun-dappled road where Minnie Mouse rumbles in on her bright yellow digger, pausing by the thickets to gather a harvest of pretend berries for a whimsical morning breakfast.
